| data.activity_id | string | no |  | A unique ID that identifies different activities. |
| data.status | string | no |  | Activity status. Possible values: - DRAFT: Promotion activities with this status are not available to TikTok users. - NOT_START: Promotion activities with this status are not available to TikTok users until the set activity start time. - ONGOING: Promotion ... |
| data.title | string | no |  | Activity name set by the merchant. |
| data.total_count | integer:int32 | no |  | The number of items in this request. When `product_level==PRODUCT`, it's the number of products; when `product_level==VARIATION`, it's the number of SKUs across products. |
| data.update_time | integer:int64 | no |  | Latest update time. UNIX timestamp. |
| message | string | no |  | The success or failure messages returned in API response. Reasons of failure will be described in the message. |
| request_id | string | no |  | Request log. |

## promotion_v202406

| method | path | summary |
| --- | --- | --- |
| POST | `/promotion/202406/coupons/search` | SearchCoupons |
| GET | `/promotion/202406/coupons/{coupon_id}` | GetCoupon |

### POST /promotion/202406/coupons/search

- Summary: SearchCoupons
- Operation ID: `-`
- Description: Use this API to get a list of coupons, narrowed down further based on search parameters. The coupons are created in Seller Center or Seller App. For further details of the specific coupon, use `GET Coupon`.
- Request schema: `Promotion_202406_SearchCoupons_RequestBody`
- Response schema: `Promotion_202406_SearchCoupons_Response`

#### Parameters

| name | in | required | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| page_token | query | no | string | Page cursor. Omitting this parameter will return the first page of results. |
| page_size | query | no | integer:int32 | The number of coupons returned in one page of results. The range is `[1, 100]`, with a default value of `50`. |
| shop_cipher | query | no | string |  |
| x-tts-access-token | header | yes | string |  |
| Content-Type | header | yes | string | Allowed type: application/json |

#### Request Fields

| field | type | required | schema | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| display_type | array<string> | no |  | The display type of coupons. | display_type[] | string | no |  |  |
| status | array<string> | no |  | Coupon's promotion status. Values: - `NOT_START`: Not available to TikTok users until the coupon's configured start time. - `ONGOING`: Available to TikTok users. - `EXPIRED`: Not available to TikTok users because it has expired. - `DEACTIVATED`: Deactivated... |
| status[] | string | no |  |  |
| title_keyword | string | no |  | Keywords in the coupon titles to search for. The range of the length is `(0, 100]`. Fuzzy matching not supported. |

| field | type | required | schema | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| code | integer:int32 | no |  | The success or failure status code returned in API response. |
| data | Promotion_202406_SearchCoupons_Response_Data | no | Promotion_202406_SearchCoupons_Response_Data |  |
| data.coupons | array<Promotion_202406_SearchCoupons_Response_Data_Coupons> | no |  | Coupons. |

| data.coupons[].claim_duration.start_time | integer:int32 | no |  | The UNIX timestamp from which a shopper can claim the coupon. |
| data.coupons[].create_time | integer:int64 | no |  | The UNIX timestamp of when the coupon was created. |
| data.coupons[].creation_source | string | no |  | The system where the coupon is created: - `SELLER_CENTER`: Created via the Promotions section of TikTok Seller Center. - `SELLER_APP`: Created via the TikTok Seller Mobile App. - `TTS_CRM`: Created via the TikTok Shop CRM. |
| data.coupons[].discount | Promotion_202406_SearchCoupons_Response_Data_Coupons_Discount | no | Promotion_202406_SearchCoupons_Response_Data_Coupons_Discount |  |
| data.coupons[].discount.max_discount | Promotion_202406_SearchCoupons_Response_Data_Coupons_Discount_MaxDiscount | no | Promotion_202406_SearchCoupons_Response_Data_Coupons_Discount_MaxDiscount |  |
| data.coupons[].discount.max_discount.amount | string | no |  | Monetary value of the `max_discount`. |
| data.coupons[].discount.max_discount.currency | string | no |  | Currency of the `max_discount`. |

| data.coupons[].discount.reduction_amount.amount | string | no |  | Monetary value of an `AMOUNT_OFF` discount. |
| data.coupons[].discount.reduction_amount.currency | string | no |  | Currency of the discount amount. |
| data.coupons[].discount.type | string | no |  | The type of discount offered by the coupon. Possible enumerations are: - `AMOUNT_OFF`: Reduces the final price of the item by the specified `reduction_amount` - `PERCENT_OFF`: Reduces the final price by the specified `percentage`. |
| data.coupons[].display_type | string | no |  | The display type of coupons. | data.coupons[].id | string | no |  | A unique ID that identifies different coupons. |
| data.coupons[].product_scope | string | no |  | The range of the products which the coupon applies to. | data.coupons[].target_buyer_segment | string | no |  | The target buyer segment of the coupon. Possible enumerations are: - `ALL`: May be discovered and claimed by all TTS buyers. - `NEW`:Customers who have never purchased from your shop. - `REPEAT_CUSTOMERS`: People who have previously placed orders in your sh... |
| data.coupons[].threshold | Promotion_202406_SearchCoupons_Response_Data_Coupons_Threshold | no | Promotion_202406_SearchCoupons_Response_Data_Coupons_Threshold |  |
| data.coupons[].threshold.min_spend | Promotion_202406_SearchCoupons_Response_Data_Coupons_Threshold_MinSpend | no | Promotion_202406_SearchCoupons_Response_Data_Coupons_Threshold_MinSpend |  |
| data.coupons[].threshold.min_spend.amount | string | no |  | Monetary value of the `min_spend`. |
| data.coupons[].threshold.min_spend.currency | string | no |  | Currency of the `min_spend` threshold. |
| data.coupons[].threshold.type | string | no |  | The type of purchase criteria. Possible enumerations are: - `NONE`: No minimum purchase criteria. - `MIN_SPEND`: Buyer must meet or exceed the minimum spend criteria with eligible products. |
| data.coupons[].title | string | no |  | Seller-specified title of the coupon. |
| data.coupons[].update_time | integer:int64 | no |  | The UNIX timestamp of when the coupon was updated. |
| data.coupons[].usage_limits | Promotion_202406_SearchCoupons_Response_Data_Coupons_UsageLimits | no | Promotion_202406_SearchCoupons_Response_Data_Coupons_UsageLimits |  |
| data.coupons[].usage_limits.redemption_limit | integer:int32 | no |  | The number of times that a claimed coupon can be redeemed across all buyers. (Only available in ID, MY, PH, TH, SG, VN regions.) |
| data.coupons[].usage_limits.single_buyer_claim_limit | integer:int32 | no |  | The number of times a single buyer can claim the coupon. |
| data.coupons[].usage_limits.total_claim_limit | integer:int32 | no |  | The total number of claims allowed for the coupon across all buyers. |
| data.next_page_token | string | no |  | Page cursor for next request. The last page returns "". |
| data.total_count | integer:int32 | no |  | Total coupon count. |

| request_id | string | no |  | Request log. |

### GET /promotion/202406/coupons/{coupon_id}

- Summary: GetCoupon
- Operation ID: `-`
- Description: Use this API to get the full details of a coupon matching the {coupon_id}.

### POST /return_refund/202309/cancellations

- Summary: CancelOrder
- Operation ID: `-`
- Description: Use this API to cancel an order on behalf of a seller. In the US and UK markets, when an item is out of stock, partial cancellation on the single item level is supported by this API.
- Request schema: `ReturnRefund_202309_CancelOrder_RequestBody`
- Response schema: `ReturnRefund_202309_CancelOrder_Response`

#### Parameters

| name | in | required | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| shop_cipher | query | no | string |  |
| x-tts-access-token | header | yes | string |  |
| Content-Type | header | yes | string | Allowed type: application/json |

#### Request Fields

| field | type | required | schema | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| cancel_reason | string | no |  | Reason to cancel the order Please see "Return API Overview" for a list of reasons a seller can select to cancel the order. |
| order_id | string | no |  | TikTok Shop order id |
| order_line_item_ids | array<string> | no |  | List of order line item ids to cancel. In the US and UK markets, when an item is out of stock, partial cancellation on the single item level is supported. To initiate a partial cancellation, specify the item's order line id. |
| order_line_item_ids[] | string | no |  |  |
| skus | array<ReturnRefund_202309_CancelOrder_RequestBody_Skus> | no |  | List of SKU to cancel |
| skus[].quantity | integer:int64 | no |  | The quantity of SKU to cancel |
| skus[].sku_id | string | no |  | The SKU id to cancel |

#### Response Fields

| field | type | required | schema | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| code | integer:int32 | no |  | The success or failure status code returned in API response. |
| data | ReturnRefund_202309_CancelOrder_Response_Data | no | ReturnRefund_202309_CancelOrder_Response_Data |  |
| data.cancel_id | string | no |  | The identifier of a specific order cancellation. |
| data.cancel_status | string | no |  | The status of an order cancellation request. Available values: - CANCELLATION_REQUEST_PENDING - CANCELLATION_REQUEST_SUCCESS - CANCELLATION_REQUEST_CANCEL - CANCELLATION_REQUEST_COMPLETE |
| message | string | no |  | The success or failure messages returned in API response. Reasons of failure will be described in the message. |
| request_id | string | no |  | Request log. |

### POST /return_refund/202309/cancellations/search

- Summary: SearchCancellations
- Operation ID: `-`
- Description: Use this API to search and retrieve one or more order cancellations.
- Request schema: `ReturnRefund_202309_SearchCancellations_RequestBody`
- Response schema: `ReturnRefund_202309_SearchCancellations_Response`

#### Parameters

| name | in | required | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| sort_field | query | no | string | The returned results will be sorted by the specified field. Default: create_time Possible values: - create_time - update_time Specify the order for sorting the returned results by using the sort_order parameter. |
| sort_order | query | no | string | The sort order for the sort_field parameter. Default: ASC Possible values: - ASC: Ascending order - DESC: Descending order |
| page_size | query | no | string | The number of results to be returned per page. Default: 10. Valid range: [1-50]. |
| page_token | query | no | string | An opaque token used to retrieve the next page of a paginated result set. Retrieve this value from the result of the next_page_token from a previous response. It is not needed for the first page. |
| shop_cipher | query | no | string |  |
| x-tts-access-token | header | yes | string |  |
| Content-Type | header | yes | string | Allowed type: application/json |

#### Request Fields

| field | type | required | schema | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| buyer_user_ids | array<string> | no |  | List of TikTok Shop buyer user IDs. |
| buyer_user_ids[] | string | no |  |  |
| cancel_ids | array<string> | no |  | List of order cancellations IDs. |
| cancel_ids[] | string | no |  |  |
| cancel_status | array<string> | no |  | List of order cancellation statuses. Possible values: - CANCELLATION_REQUEST_PENDING - CANCELLATION_REQUEST_SUCCESS - CANCELLATION_REQUEST_CANCEL - CANCELLATION_REQUEST_COMPLETE Please see "API Overview" for more information about these statuses. |
| cancel_status[] | string | no |  |  |
| cancel_types | array<string> | no |  | List of order cancellation types. Possible values: - CANCEL: Cancel by seller or system. - BUYER_CANCEL: Cancel by buyer. Need to be approved by seller or system. |
| cancel_types[] | string | no |  |  |
| create_time_ge | integer:int64 | no |  | Filter cancellations to show only orders that have been created after a specified date and time. Unix timestamp. |
| create_time_lt | integer:int64 | no |  | Filter cancellations to show only orders that have been created before a specified date and time. Unix timestamp. |
| locale | string | no |  | The BCP-47 locale codes for displaying the order, delimited by commas. Default: en-US Refer to [Locale codes](678e3a47bae28f030a8c7523) for the list of supported locale codes. |
| order_ids | array<string> | no |  | List of TikTok Shop order IDs. |
| order_ids[] | string | no |  |  |
| update_time_ge | integer:int64 | no |  | Filter cancellations to show only orders that have been updated after a specified date and time. Unix timestamp. |
| update_time_lt | integer:int64 | no |  | Filter cancellations to show only orders that have been updated before a specified date and time. Unix timestamp. |

| field | type | required | schema | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| code | integer:int32 | no |  | The success or failure status code returned in API response. |
| data | ReturnRefund_202309_SearchCancellations_Response_Data | no | ReturnRefund_202309_SearchCancellations_Response_Data |  |
| data.cancellations | array<ReturnRefund_202309_SearchCancellations_Response_Data_Cancellations> | no |  | List of order cancellations. |
| data.cancellations[].cancel_id | string | no |  | The identifier of a specific order cancellation. |

| data.cancellations[].cancel_line_items[].refund_amount.currency | string | no |  | Refund currency. |
| data.cancellations[].cancel_line_items[].refund_amount.refund_shipping_fee | string | no |  | Shipping fee refund amount to the buyer. |

| data.cancellations[].cancel_line_items[].refund_amount.retail_delivery_fee | string | no |  | Retail delivery fee takes effect once platform GMV exceeds 500,000 USD, according to Colorado (US) compliance rules. |
| data.cancellations[].cancel_line_items[].seller_sku | string | no |  | SKU name defined by the seller. |
| data.cancellations[].cancel_line_items[].sku_id | string | no |  | SKU ID. |
| data.cancellations[].cancel_line_items[].sku_name | string | no |  | SKU name. |
| data.cancellations[].cancel_reason | string | no |  | Order cancellation reason. |
| data.cancellations[].cancel_reason_text | string | no |  | Order cancellation reason, localized to another language. You can change language using the locale field in the request parameter. |
| data.cancellations[].cancel_status | string | no |  | Order cancellation status. Possible values: - CANCELLATION_REQUEST_PENDING - CANCELLATION_REQUEST_SUCCESS - CANCELLATION_REQUEST_CANCELLED - CANCELLATION_REQUEST_COMPLETE |
| data.cancellations[].cancel_type | string | no |  | Order cancellation type. Possible values: - CANCEL: Cancel by seller or system. - BUYER_CANCEL: Cancel by buyer. Need to be approved by seller or system. |
| data.cancellations[].create_time | integer:int64 | no |  | Order cancellation create time. Unix timestamp. |
| data.cancellations[].order_id | string | no |  | TikTok Shop order ID. Contains multiple order line item IDs. |
| data.cancellations[].refund_amount | ReturnRefund_202309_SearchCancellations_Response_Data_Cancellations_RefundAmount | no | ReturnRefund_202309_SearchCancellations_Response_Data_Cancellations_RefundAmount |  |
| data.cancellations[].refund_amount.buyer_service_fee | string | no |  | Only for the ID market. Platform will charge the buyer a service fee depending on the scenario. |
| data.cancellations[].refund_amount.currency | string | no |  | Refund currency. |
| data.cancellations[].refund_amount.refund_shipping_fee | string | no |  | Shipping fee refund amount to the buyer. |
| data.cancellations[].refund_amount.refund_subtotal | string | no |  | Subtotal refund amount to the buyer. |
| data.cancellations[].refund_amount.refund_tax | string | no |  | Tax refund amount to the buyer. |
| data.cancellations[].refund_amount.refund_total | string | no |  | Total refund amount to the buyer. |
| data.cancellations[].refund_amount.retail_delivery_fee | string | no |  | Retail delivery fee takes effect once platform GMV exceeds $500,000 USD, according to Colorado (US) compliance rules. |

| request_id | string | no |  | Request log. |

### POST /return_refund/202309/cancellations/{cancel_id}/approve

- Summary: ApproveCancellation

- Description: Use this API to approve a buyer-initiated cancellation request identified by cancel_id. Call this API only when the cancellation request is still waiting for seller action.
- Request schema: `-`
- Response schema: `ReturnRefund_202309_ApproveCancellation_Response`

| idempotency_key | query | no | string | The idempotency key is a unique value generated by the client which the server uses to recognize a request. You may choose your own method of creating unique keys. We suggest using UUIDs, or another random string with enough entropy, to avoid collisions. Id... |
| shop_cipher | query | no | string |  |
| x-tts-access-token | header | yes | string |  |

### POST /return_refund/202309/cancellations/{cancel_id}/reject

- Summary: RejectCancellation
- Operation ID: `-`
- Description: Use this API to reject a buyer's cancellation request for the specified cancel_id. Before calling this API, make sure the request is still in a state where seller rejection is currently allowed.
- Request schema: `ReturnRefund_202309_RejectCancellation_RequestBody`
- Response schema: `ReturnRefund_202309_RejectCancellation_Response`

#### Parameters

| name | in | required | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| cancel_id | path | yes | string | The cancellation request ID to approve. Use the `cancel_id` returned by Search Cancellations. Do not pass the order ID here. |
| idempotency_key | query | no | string | The idempotency key is a unique value generated by the client which the server uses to recognize a request. You may choose your own method of creating unique keys. We suggest using UUIDs, or another random string with enough entropy, to avoid collisions. Id... |
| shop_cipher | query | no | string |  |
| x-tts-access-token | header | yes | string |  |
| Content-Type | header | yes | string | Allowed type: application/json |

#### Request Fields

| field | type | required | schema | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| comment | string | no |  | Seller's comment on rejection decision. This is where a seller will provide more information about rejecting the request. |

| images[].image_id | string | no |  | Image ID. |
| images[].mime_type | string | no |  | MIME type. |
| images[].width | integer:int32 | no |  | Image width. Unit: px |

### GET /return_refund/202309/orders/{order_id}/aftersale_eligibility

- Summary: GetAftersaleEligibility
- Operation ID: `-`
- Description: Use this API to check eligible aftersale solutions for an order, including whether the seller or buyer can initiate a refund, return, or cancel a specific order.
- Request schema: `-`
- Response schema: `ReturnRefund_202309_GetAftersaleEligibility_Response`

#### Parameters

| name | in | required | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| order_id | path | yes | string | The unique identifier for a TikTok Shop order. |
| initiate_aftersale_user | query | no | string | The type of user you would like to check aftersale options for. Default: SELLER Possible values: - SELLER - BUYER |
| shop_cipher | query | no | string |  |
| x-tts-access-token | header | yes | string |  |
| Content-Type | header | yes | string | Allowed type: application/json |

_None._

| data.sku_eligibility[].line_item_eligibility[].order_line_items_ids[] | string | no |  |  |

### POST /return_refund/202309/refunds/calculate

- Summary: CalculateRefund
- Operation ID: `-`
- Description: Use this API to check order refundable amounts.

- Response schema: `ReturnRefund_202309_CalculateRefund_Response`

| name | in | required | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |

| x-tts-access-token | header | yes | string |  |

#### Request Fields

| field | type | required | schema | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| handover_method | string | no |  | Which handover method buyer chooses to use when returning goods to seller by platform - DROP_OFF: buyer drops off the parcel in logistics service collect point. - PICKUP: logistics service picks up the buyer package. |
| order_id | string | no |  | TikTok Shop order id. |
| order_line_item_ids | array<string> | no |  | TikTok Shop order line item ids. |
| order_line_item_ids[] | string | no |  |  |
| reason_name | string | no |  | Seller's reason to create a return，all available reasons, please reference to API overview. |
| request_type | string | no |  | Request type - CANCEL - REFUND - RETURN_AND_REFUND Note: different request types are used for different aftersales scenarios. Also, different request types might have different refund amounts, because the refund amount calculation policy is different. |
| shipment_type | string | no |  | How buyer ships items to seller when in a return request, could be: - PLATFORM - BUYER_ARRANGE |
| skus | array<ReturnRefund_202309_CalculateRefund_RequestBody_Skus> | no |  | skus |
| skus[].quantity | integer:int32 | no |  | Sku id seller wants to create a return request. |
| skus[].sku_id | string | no |  | Quantity seller wants to create a return request. |

### POST /return_refund/202309/returns

- Summary: CreateReturn
- Operation ID: `-`
- Description: Use this API to initiate a return request on behalf of the buyer Sellers can reject the request, or accept and issue: -Return and Refund (buyer must send package back) -Returnless Refund (buyer can keep the item) -Partial Refund (Seller issues a partial refund, buyer can keep the item)
- Request schema: `ReturnRefund_202309_CreateReturn_RequestBody`
- Response schema: `ReturnRefund_202309_CreateReturn_Response`

#### Parameters

| name | in | required | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| idempotency_key | query | no | string | Idempotency Key |
| shop_cipher | query | no | string |  |
| x-tts-access-token | header | yes | string |  |
| Content-Type | header | yes | string | Allowed type: application/json |

#### Request Fields

| field | type | required | schema | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| currency | string | no |  | Currency for refund which should be same as currency in TikTok Ship Order. |
| handover_method | string | no |  | Which handover method buyer chooses to use when returning goods to seller by platform - DROP_OFF - PICKUP |
| order_id | string | no |  | Tiktok Shop order id |
| order_line_item_ids | array<string> | no |  | Order line ids seller wants to create return request, should use order line ids to create return request if had split parcel to multiple packages, default value is all order line ids in the order if not input order_line_ids and skus |
| order_line_item_ids[] | string | no |  |  |
| refund_total | string | no |  | Total refund amount to the buyer. The total refund amount can not exceed the refundable amount |
| return_reason | string | no |  | Seller's reason to create a return |
| return_type | string | no |  | Which type to create: - REFUND - RETURN_AND_REFUND |
| shipment_type | string | no |  | How buyer ships items to seller when in a return request, could be: - PLATFORM - BUYER_ARRANGE |
| skus | array<ReturnRefund_202309_CreateReturn_RequestBody_Skus> | no |  | Skus |
| skus[].quantity | integer:int32 | no |  | Quantity seller wants to create a return request |
| skus[].sku_id | string | no |  | Sku id seller wants to create a return request |

#### Response Fields

| field | type | required | schema | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| code | integer:int32 | no |  | The success or failure status code returned in API response. |
| data | ReturnRefund_202309_CreateReturn_Response_Data | no | ReturnRefund_202309_CreateReturn_Response_Data |  |
| data.return_id | string | no |  | The identifier of a specific return request. |
| data.return_status | string | no |  | Return status, available values: - RETURN_OR_REFUND_REQUEST_PENDING: Request is pending, needs to be approved by seller or platform - REFUND_OR_RETURN_REQUEST_REJECT: Seller rejected the request - AWAITING_BUYER_SHIP: Waiting buyer to ship items to seller, ... |
| message | string | no |  | The success or failure messages returned in API response. Reasons of failure will be described in the message. |
| request_id | string | no |  | Request log. |

### POST /return_refund/202602/cancellations

- Summary: CancelOrder
- Operation ID: `-`
- Description: Use this API to cancel an order on behalf of a seller. In the US and UK markets, when an item is out of stock, partial cancellation on the single item level is supported by this API.
- Request schema: `ReturnRefund_202602_CancelOrder_RequestBody`
- Response schema: `ReturnRefund_202602_CancelOrder_Response`

In the US and UK markets, when an item is out of stock, partial cancellation on the single item level is supported. To initiate a partial cancellation, specify the item's order line id. **Note:** This is a legacy field... |

| order_line_list[].sub_order_line_item_id | string | no |  | The sub-order-line ID inside a bundle product - Required for bundle products - Not required for non-bundle products |
| skus | array<ReturnRefund_202602_CancelOrder_RequestBody_Skus> | no |  | List of SKU to cancel |
| skus[].quantity | integer:int64 | no |  | The quantity of SKU to cancel |
| skus[].sku_id | string | no |  | The SKU id to cancel |

### GET /return_refund/202602/orders/{order_id}/aftersale_eligibility

- Summary: GetAftersaleEligibility
- Operation ID: `-`
- Description: Use this API to check eligible aftersale solutions for an order, including whether the seller or buyer can initiate a refund, return, or cancel a specific order.
- Request schema: `-`
- Response schema: `ReturnRefund_202602_GetAftersaleEligibility_Response`

#### Parameters

| name | in | required | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| order_id | path | yes | string | The unique identifier for a TikTok Shop order. |
| initiate_aftersale_user | query | no | string | The type of user you would like to check aftersale options for. Default: SELLER Possible values: - SELLER - BUYER |
| request_types | query | no | array<string> | **Optional.** Specifies which request types to query. If omitted, the API returns all request types currently available for the specified initiator. Possible values: -`CANCEL` -`REFUND` -`RETURN_AND_REFUND` |
| shop_cipher | query | no | string |  |
| x-tts-access-token | header | yes | string |  |
| Content-Type | header | yes | string | Allowed type: application/json |

#### Request Fields

_None._

#### Response Fields

| field | type | required | schema | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| code | integer:int32 | no |  | The success or failure status code returned in API response. |
| data | ReturnRefund_202602_GetAftersaleEligibility_Response_Data | no | ReturnRefund_202602_GetAftersaleEligibility_Response_Data |  |
| data.sku_eligibility | array<ReturnRefund_202602_GetAftersaleEligibility_Response_Data_SkuEligibility> | no |  | The eligible aftersale options for a given SKU. |
| data.sku_eligibility[].line_item_eligibility | array<ReturnRefund_202602_GetAftersaleEligibility_Response_Data_SkuEligibility_LineItemEligibility> | no |  | The eligible aftersale options based on order line item. |
| data.sku_eligibility[].line_item_eligibility[].available_reason_names | array<string> | no |  | A list of reason names that can be used when creating a follow-up aftersale request. |
| data.sku_eligibility[].line_item_eligibility[].available_reason_names[] | string | no |  |  |
| data.sku_eligibility[].line_item_eligibility[].eligible | boolean | no |  | Use this field to recognize whether an item is eligible for an aftersale request: - `TRUE` - `FALSE` |
| data.sku_eligibility[].line_item_eligibility[].ineligible_code | integer:int32 | no |  | The reason code for an ineligible aftersale request. |
| data.sku_eligibility[].line_item_eligibility[].ineligible_reason | string | no |  | The reason for an ineligible aftersale request. |
| data.sku_eligibility[].line_item_eligibility[].order_line_items_ids | array<string> | no |  | Specifies the order line IDs. **Note:** This is a legacy field for new integrations use `order_line_list` instead. |
| data.sku_eligibility[].line_item_eligibility[].order_line_items_ids[] | string | no |  |  |
| data.sku_eligibility[].line_item_eligibility[].order_line_list | array<ReturnRefund_202602_GetAftersaleEligibility_Response_Data_SkuEligibility_LineItemEligibility_OrderLineList> | no |  | Specifies the order line items. **Note:** This field is completely backward compatible with the previous `order_line_items_ids` field. |
| data.sku_eligibility[].line_item_eligibility[].order_line_list[].order_line_item_id | string | no |  | The corresponding order line ID |
| data.sku_eligibility[].line_item_eligibility[].order_line_list[].sub_order_line_item_id | string | no |  | The sub-order-line ID inside a bundle product - Required for bundle products - Not required for non-bundle products |
| data.sku_eligibility[].line_item_eligibility[].request_type | string | no |  | Aftersale request type. Possible values: - `CANCEL` - `RETURN` - `RETURN_AND_REFUND` |
| data.sku_eligibility[].sku_id | string | no |  | SKU ID. |
| message | string | no |  | The success or failure messages returned in API response. Reasons of failure will be described in the message. |
| request_id | string | no |  | Request log. |

### POST /return_refund/202602/refunds/calculate

- Summary: CalculateRefund
- Operation ID: `-`
- Description: Use this API to estimate the refundable amount for a cancellation, refund, or return-and-refund request. The estimate depends on the selected request_type, reason_name, and targeted items. For return-and-refund scenarios, the estimate can also depend on the selected return shipping method.
- Request schema: `ReturnRefund_202602_CalculateRefund_RequestBody`
- Response schema: `ReturnRefund_202602_CalculateRefund_Response`

#### Parameters

| name | in | required | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| shop_cipher | query | no | string |  |
| x-tts-access-token | header | yes | string |  |
| Content-Type | header | yes | string | Allowed type: application/json |

#### Request Fields

| field | type | required | schema | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| handover_method | string | no |  | Which handover method buyer chooses to use when returning goods to seller by platform - `DROP_OFF`: buyer drops off the parcel in logistics service collect point. - `PICKUP`: logistics service picks up the buyer package. |
| order_id | string | no |  | TikTok Shop order id. |
| order_line_item_ids | array<string> | no |  | TikTok Shop order line item ids. |
| order_line_item_ids[] | string | no |  |  |
| order_line_list | array<ReturnRefund_202602_CalculateRefund_RequestBody_OrderLineList> | no |  | This new parameter is completely backward compatible with the previous order_line_items_ids field and in addition this supports bundle order splitting, use the sub items accordingly |
| order_line_list[].order_line_item_id | string | no |  | The corresponding order line ID |
| order_line_list[].sub_order_line_item_id | string | no |  | The sub-order-line ID inside a bundle product - Required for bundle products - Not required for non-bundle products |
| reason_name | string | no |  | Seller's reason to create a return. Use the [Get Aftersale Eligibility](https://partner.tiktokshop.com/docv2/page/69c3070c66dee40493fdfe2e) endpoint to retrieve the currently available reasons, and submit the returned reason name in this field. |
| request_type | string | no |  | Request type - CANCEL - REFUND - RETURN_AND_REFUND Note: different request types are used for different aftersales scenarios. Also, different request types might have different refund amounts, because the refund amount calculation policy is different. |
| shipment_type | string | no |  | How buyer ships items to seller when in a return request, could be: - `PLATFORM` - `BUYER_ARRANGE` |
| skus | array<ReturnRefund_202602_CalculateRefund_RequestBody_Skus> | no |  | skus |
| skus[].quantity | integer:int32 | no |  | Sku id seller wants to create a return request. |
| skus[].sku_id | string | no |  | Quantity seller wants to create a return request. |

#### Response Fields

| field | type | required | schema | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| code | integer:int32 | no |  | The success or failure status code returned in API response. |
| data | ReturnRefund_202602_CalculateRefund_Response_Data | no | ReturnRefund_202602_CalculateRefund_Response_Data |  |
| data.order_refund_amount | ReturnRefund_202602_CalculateRefund_Response_Data_OrderRefundAmount | no | ReturnRefund_202602_CalculateRefund_Response_Data_OrderRefundAmount |  |
| data.order_refund_amount.currency | string | no |  | Currency for payment. |
| data.order_refund_amount.refund_shipping_fee | string | no |  | Shipping fee refund to buyer. |
| data.order_refund_amount.refund_subtotal | string | no |  | Total price of item be returned. |
| data.order_refund_amount.refund_tax | string | no |  | Tax returned to buyer. |
| data.order_refund_amount.refund_total | string | no |  | Total amount refund to buyer. |
| data.order_refund_amount.retail_delivery_fee | string | no |  | Retail delivery fee takes effect once platform GMV exceeds 500,000 USD, according to US Colorado states' compliance rules. |
| message | string | no |  | The success or failure messages returned in API response. Reasons of failure will be described in the message. |
| request_id | string | no |  | Request log. |

### POST /return_refund/202602/returns

- Summary: CreateReturn
- Operation ID: `-`
- Description: Use this API to create a seller-initiated aftersales request for an order on behalf of the buyer. Supported request types are `REFUND` and `RETURN_AND_REFUND`. Before submitting the request, confirm that the order is eligible for the selected aftersales type and return method. After the request is created, use the relevant search, status, or review API to continue the aftersales workflow.
- Request schema: `ReturnRefund_202602_CreateReturn_RequestBody`
- Response schema: `ReturnRefund_202602_CreateReturn_Response`

#### Parameters

| name | in | required | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| idempotency_key | query | no | string | Idempotency Key |
| shop_cipher | query | no | string |  |
| x-tts-access-token | header | yes | string |  |
| Content-Type | header | yes | string | Allowed type: application/json |

#### Request Fields

| field | type | required | schema | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| currency | string | no |  | Currency for refund which should be same as currency in TikTok Ship Order. |
| handover_method | string | no |  | **Optional**. Only applicable when shipment_type is `PLATFORM`. Specifies the handover method the buyer uses to return the item through platform-supported shipping. Supported values: - `DROP_OFF` - `PICKUP` |
| order_id | string | no |  | Tiktok Shop order id |
| order_line_item_ids | array<string> | no |  | Specifies the order line IDs to include in the return request. If the order has been split into multiple packages, use order line IDs to create the return request. If neither order_line_item_ids nor skus is provided, the default is all order lines in the or... |
| order_line_item_ids[] | string | no |  |  |
| order_line_list | array<ReturnRefund_202602_CreateReturn_RequestBody_OrderLineList> | no |  | Specifies the line items to include in the return request and supports bundle order splitting. In one request, use either `order_line_item_id` for normal order lines or `sub_order_line_item_id` for bundle sub-items. **Note:** This field is completely backwa... |
| order_line_list[].order_line_item_id | string | no |  | The corresponding order line ID |
| order_line_list[].sub_order_line_item_id | string | no |  | The sub-order-line ID inside a bundle product - Required for bundle products - Not required for non-bundle products |
| refund_total | string | no |  | Total refund amount to the buyer. The total refund amount can not exceed the refundable amount |
| return_reason | string | no |  | Seller's reason to create a return |
| return_type | string | no |  | Which type to create: - REFUND - RETURN_AND_REFUND |
| shipment_type | string | no |  | Specifies the return shipping method the buyer uses to send items back to the seller in a return request. Supported values: - `PLATFORM` - `BUYER_ARRANGE` |
| skus | array<ReturnRefund_202602_CreateReturn_RequestBody_Skus> | no |  | Skus |
| skus[].quantity | integer:int32 | no |  | Quantity seller wants to create a return request |
| skus[].sku_id | string | no |  | Sku id seller wants to create a return request |

#### Response Fields

| field | type | required | schema | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| code | integer:int32 | no |  | The success or failure status code returned in API response. |
| data | ReturnRefund_202602_CreateReturn_Response_Data | no | ReturnRefund_202602_CreateReturn_Response_Data |  |
| data.return_id | string | no |  | The identifier of a specific return request. |
| data.return_status | string | no |  | Return status, available values: - RETURN_OR_REFUND_REQUEST_PENDING: Request is pending, needs to be approved by seller or platform - REFUND_OR_RETURN_REQUEST_REJECT: Seller rejected the request - AWAITING_BUYER_SHIP: Waiting buyer to ship items to seller, ... |
| message | string | no |  | The success or failure messages returned in API response. Reasons of failure will be described in the message. |
| request_id | string | no |  | Request log. |

### POST /return_refund/202602/returns/search

- Summary: SearchReturns
- Operation ID: `-`
- Description: Use this API to search returns by return ID, order ID, buyer, return type, return status, arbitration status, or create/update time window. The response is paginated. To retrieve the next page, pass the previous `next_page_token` as `page_token` and keep all other filters and sorting unchanged.
- Request schema: `ReturnRefund_202602_SearchReturns_RequestBody`
- Response schema: `ReturnRefund_202602_SearchReturns_Response`

#### Parameters

| name | in | required | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| sort_field | query | no | string | The returned results will be sorted by the specified field. **Default**: `create_time` Possible values: - `create_time` - `update_time` Specify the order for sorting the returned results by using the `sort_order` parameter. |
| sort_order | query | no | string | The sort order for the `sort_field` parameter. **Default**: `ASC` Possible values: - `ASC`: Ascending order - `DESC`: Descending order |
| page_size | query | no | string | The number of results to be returned per page. **Default**: 10. Valid range: [10-50]. |
| page_token | query | no | string | An opaque token used to retrieve the next page of a paginated result set. Retrieve this value from the result of the `next_page_token` from a previous response. It is not needed for the first page. |
| shop_cipher | query | no | string |  |
| x-tts-access-token | header | yes | string |  |
| Content-Type | header | yes | string | Allowed type: application/json |

| field | type | required | schema | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| arbitration_status | array<string> | no |  | List of arbitration statuses. Available values: - `IN_PROGRESS`: The TikTok Shop platform operator is processing arbitration. Platform may request additional information from the seller. - `SUPPORT_BUYER`: The platform operator supports buyer. - `SUPPORT_SE... |
| arbitration_status[] | string | no |  |  |
| buyer_user_ids | array<string> | no |  | List of TikTok Shop buyer user IDs. |
| buyer_user_ids[] | string | no |  |  |
| create_time_ge | integer:int64 | no |  | Filter returns to show only those that are created on or after the specified date and time. Unix timestamp. Note: `create_time_ge` and `create_time_le` together constitute the creation time filter condition. - If `create_time_ge` is filled but `create_time_... |
| create_time_lt | integer:int64 | no |  | Filter returns to show only those that are created before the specified date and time. Unix timestamp. Refer to notes in `create_time_ge` for more usage information. |
| locale | string | no |  | The BCP-47 locale codes for displaying the returns, delimited by commas. Default: `en` Refer to [Locale codes](678e3a47bae28f030a8c7523) for the list of supported locale codes. |
| order_ids | array<string> | no |  | List of TikTok Shop order IDs. |

| return_ids[] | string | no |  |  |

The request is pending review by seller or system. - `REFUND_OR_RETURN_REQUEST_REJECT`: The return or refund request was rejected.... |
| return_status[] | string | no |  |  |
| return_types | array<string> | no |  | List of return types. Available values: - `REFUND` - `RETURN_AND_REFUND` - `REPLACEMENT` |

| update_time_lt | integer:int64 | no |  | Filter returns to show only those that are created before the specified date and time. Unix timestamp. Refer to notes in `create_time_ge` for more usage information. |

### POST /return_refund/202603/aftersales/review

- Summary: ReviewAftersales
- Operation ID: `-`
- Description: API used to review (approve, reject, or offer alternatives to) a buyer's return request.
- Request schema: `ReturnRefund_202603_ReviewAftersales_RequestBody`

### POST /return_refund/202603/rma/search

- Summary: SearchReturnMerchandiseAuthorization
- Operation ID: `-`
- Description: Used to search for RMA request level data
- Request schema: `ReturnRefund_202603_SearchReturnMerchandiseAuthorization_RequestBody`
- Response schema: `ReturnRefund_202603_SearchReturnMerchandiseAuthorization_Response`

#### Parameters

| name | in | required | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| locale | query | no | string | The BCP-47 locale codes for displaying purposes. Default: `en` Refer to [Locale codes](678e3a47bae28f030a8c7523) for the list of supported locale codes. |
| shop_cipher | query | no | string |  |
| x-tts-access-token | header | yes | string |  |
| Content-Type | header | yes | string | Allowed type: application/json |

#### Request Fields

| field | type | required | schema | description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| data_whitelist | array<string> | no |  | Whitelist of field names to return additional data in the response. Possible values: Possible values: - LINE_ITEMS - SKU_RETURN_REQUESTS |
| data_whitelist[] | string | no |  |  |
| filters | ReturnRefund_202603_SearchReturnMerchandiseAuthorization_RequestBody_Filters | no | ReturnRefund_202603_SearchReturnMerchandiseAuthorization_RequestBody_Filters |  |
| filters.aftersales_request_ids | array<string> | no |  | Restricts results to the specified aftersales requests. |
| filters.aftersales_request_ids[] | string | no |  |  |
| filters.main_order_ids | array<string> | no |  | Restricts results to the specified main (forward) order IDs. |
| filters.main_order_ids[] | string | no |  |  |
| filters.package_ids | array<string> | no |  | Restricts results to the specified packages. |
| filters.package_ids[] | string | no |  |  |
| filters.rma_ids | array<string> | no |  | Restricts results to the specified RMA data. |
| filters.rma_ids[] | string | no |  |  |
| filters.statuses | array<string> | no |  | Restricts results to the specified RMA data statuses. Possible values: - INIT - AWAITING_BUYER_RETURN - RETURN_IN_TRANSIT - PENDING_PACKAGE_REVIEW - PACKAGE_REVIEW_COMPLETED |
| filters.statuses[] | string | no |  |  |

| filters.time.update_time | ReturnRefund_202603_SearchReturnMerchandiseAuthorization_RequestBody_Filters_Time_UpdateTime | no | ReturnRefund_202603_SearchReturnMerchandiseAuthorization_RequestBody_Filters_Time_UpdateTime |  |

| pagination | ReturnRefund_202603_SearchReturnMerchandiseAuthorization_RequestBody_Pagination | no | ReturnRefund_202603_SearchReturnMerchandiseAuthorization_RequestBody_Pagination |  |

## seller_v202309

| method | path | summary |
| --- | --- | --- |
| GET | `/seller/202309/permissions` | GetSellerPermissions |
| GET | `/seller/202309/shops` | GetActiveShops |

### GET /seller/202309/permissions

- Summary: GetSellerPermissions
- Operation ID: `-`
- Description: Retrieves the cross-border operations that a cross-border seller is permitted to perform. You can use this API prior to listing products to check whether a seller has the ability to list global products. Target seller: Cross-border sellers
- Request schema: `-`
- Response schema: `Seller_202309_GetSellerPermissions_Response`

#### Parameters

### GET /seller/202601/shop_groups

- Summary: GetShopGroup

### POST /supply_chain/202309/packages/sync

- Summary: ConfirmPackageShipment
- Operation ID: `-`
- Description: This API enables a warehouse service provider to send package shipment information for an order. Only warehouse service providers who have been certified by the platform have permission to access this interface.
- Request schema: `SupplyChain_202309_ConfirmPackageShipment_RequestBody`
- Response schema: `SupplyChain_202309_ConfirmPackageShipment_Response`

| packages[].weight.value | integer:int64 | no |  | Package weight value |
| packages[].wms_order_id | string | no |  | Warehouse Management System order number |
